brands.name: Daikin
brands.notes: Premium brand (Daikin Fit inverter platform + DM96VC/DM97MC Goodman-platform rebadges + Daikin One+ smart thermostat)

brands.name: Goodman
brands.notes: Primary residential furnace/heat pump platform (Daikin-owned since 2012). Shares most hardware with Amana.

brands.name: Amana
brands.notes: Value sibling to Goodman, Daikin-owned. Largely Goodman-platform aliases (AMVC96 = GMVC96 hardware).

structural_overview: Daikin Industries US residential diagnostics span four distinct platforms with fundamentally different lineages: (1) GOODMAN/AMANA LEGACY SINGLE-LED — pre-2012 furnaces with single red LED flash codes (1-8 flashes) plus steady-on / off / continuous-rapid polarity states. Published flash legend is concise — steady on normal, 2 flashes pressure switch stuck closed, 3 flashes pressure switch stuck open, 4 flashes open high limit, 5 flashes flame without gas valve, 6 flashes open rollout or open fuse, 7 flashes low flame signal, 8 flashes check igniter or improper grounding, continuous flashes reversed polarity. (2) MODERN GOODMAN/AMANA/DAIKIN DUAL 7-SEGMENT IFC — DC96VC/DM96VC and aliases (GMVC96, AMVC96, DM96SC, DM97MC) use E-code namespace (E0-EA) plus d-codes for shared data (d0, d4) plus b-codes for blower motor faults (b0-b5, b7, b9). Hardware redesign from legacy, not a carry-forward. (3) COMFORTNET / DAIKIN ONE+ COMMUNICATING ECOSYSTEM — CTK04 ComfortNet thermostat and Daikin One+ smart thermostat surface equipment faults through 'Call for Service' icon and 'Check Furnace' scroll text rather than publishing a rich thermostat-native code dictionary. Public diagnostics menus confirm last-6-faults storage with consecutive repeats capped at 3. Daikin One+ exposes Error History under System Optimization menu with color-coded alerts (white = not critical, yellow = critical). (4) DAIKIN FIT INVERTER OUTDOOR — DZ17VSA/DZ18VSA/DX17/DX18/DZ20 platform uses distinct numeric namespace E25-E59 + B0/B9 + D0-D4 + thermostat-only E11 via 3-digit onboard mode display with LEARN/RECALL/TEST buttons. Japanese-style L-codes and U-codes (L1, L3, L5, U0, U2, U4) were NOT found in US residential Fit public documentation — that namespace belongs to Daikin's commercial/mini-split product lines, not US Fit residential. Cross-platform code conflicts are real and dangerous: legacy '3 flashes' (pressure switch stuck open) vs modern E3 (open primary/aux/rollout limit) are completely different fault families; modern furnace E4 (flame sensed no call for heat) vs Fit E41 (low refrigerant) share the E-prefix letter but have nothing else in common.

field_quirks.quirk: Ignition lockout timing: 3 tries, 1-hour auto-reset, manual reset by thermostat off 5-20 seconds or power off minimum 5 seconds
field_quirks.applies_to: DC96VC/DM96VC and Goodman/Amana modern 7-segment IFC family
field_quirks.reason: Published reset procedure in DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions p.41: control allows 3 total ignition tries before lockout; auto-reset after 1 hour; manual reset requires thermostat off more than 5 seconds AND less than 20 seconds, or power off for at least 5 seconds. Getting the manual reset window wrong — either too short or too long — fails to clear the lockout and technician is left troubleshooting a phantom repeat fault.

field_quirks.quirk: Modern furnace diagnostics menu stores last 6 faults with consecutive repeats capped at 3
field_quirks.reason: Accessible via Fault 1 through Fault 6 menu entries on the dual 7-segment board. CLEAR = YES erases stored history. Consecutively repeated faults are shown a maximum of 3 times to prevent a single recurring event from consuming all six memory slots. A fault history showing the same code three times consecutively means it repeated at least 3 times — probably more, but 3 is the ceiling.

field_quirks.quirk: Daikin Fit onboard 3-digit display uses Screen 0-4 mode workflow via LEARN/RECALL/TEST buttons
field_quirks.applies_to: Daikin Fit outdoor inverter (DZ17VSA, DZ18VSA, DX17, DX18, DZ20, DX20) — all models with the 3-digit outdoor PCB display
field_quirks.reason: Screen 0 shows most recent fault. Hold RECALL 5 seconds from Screen 0 to reach Screen 1 (fault history — last 6 faults). Hold RECALL 5 seconds from Screen 1 to reach Screen 2 (monitoring). Hold RECALL 5 seconds from Screen 2 to reach Screen 3 (setting mode 1). Hold RECALL + TEST simultaneously 5 seconds to reach Screen 4 (setting mode 2). Press LEARN to return to Screen 0. This is a distinct service workflow that doesn't appear on any Goodman/Amana platform.

field_quirks.quirk: LEARN button reset timing differs between Daikin Fit outdoor (~5 seconds) and ComfortNet indoor (~2 seconds)
field_quirks.applies_to: Network reset on Daikin Fit outdoor vs ComfortNet communicating air handler/modular blower indoor controls
field_quirks.reason: Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S p.38 specifies ~5-second LEARN button hold to reset the network. Goodman ComfortNet modular blower public manual p.12 specifies ~2-second hold. Using the wrong timing either fails to trigger reset (too short) or moves the control into a different state like out-of-box reset (too long). Platform-specific timing — not a universal ComfortNet standard.

field_quirks.quirk: L-codes and U-codes (Japanese-style) are NOT part of US residential Daikin Fit namespace
field_quirks.applies_to: US residential Daikin Fit DZ17VSA/DZ18VSA/DX17/DX18/DZ20/DX20 models
field_quirks.reason: Public US residential Fit documentation (3P515859-5S and related) exposes E-prefixed numeric codes (E25-E59, Eb0, Eb9, Ed0-Ed4, E11) — NOT the L1/L3/L5/U0/U2/U4 namespace common to Daikin's Japanese commercial and US mini-split (DZ series ductless) product lines. If a technician encounters an L-code or U-code, it's either a Daikin mini-split or commercial product, not US residential Fit. Do not publish L/U records to this dataset unless confirmed by a US-residential OEM public document.

field_quirks.quirk: Daikin Fit network troubleshooting includes DS1 dip-switch toggle as last-resort step
field_quirks.applies_to: Daikin Fit outdoor inverter (DZ17VSA, DZ18VSA, DX17, DX18, DZ20, DX20)
field_quirks.reason: Public Fit outdoor installation manual specifies a network troubleshooting workflow: after wiring changes or DS1 dip-switch changes, apply power and see if the error code cleared. If not, confirm low-voltage wiring correct, check for miswiring including terminal 1 and 2 reversed, inspect wire damage, perform continuity checks, and THEN change both DS1 dip switches on the outdoor control board to the opposite position. This DS1 toggle is a platform-specific last-resort reset that doesn't exist on Goodman/Amana furnace platforms.

field_quirks.quirk: Daikin One+ wiring validation: 0.6 VDC between Data 1 and 2, 24 VDC between R and C; reversed power makes PWR LED flash green
field_quirks.applies_to: Daikin One+ S21 smart thermostat installations
field_quirks.reason: Public S21 installation guide specifies these voltage checkpoints: 0.6 VDC between Data 1 and Data 2 terminals (communication bus) and 24 VDC between R and C (thermostat power). If power wires are reversed, the PWR LED flashes GREEN — a specific field indicator a technician can read without a meter. This is the only thermostat-side wiring fault indicator publicly documented for the Daikin One+ platform.

field_quirks.quirk: Legacy Goodman/Amana ignition control auto-resets after one hour — anchored to GMH8/GMS8/GDH8/GDS8 public installation instructions
field_quirks.applies_to: Goodman GMH8, GMS8, GDH8, GDS8, and similar pre-2012 single-LED legacy furnaces
field_quirks.reason: Public Goodman GMH8/GMS8/GDH8/GDS8 installation instructions spell out that the integrated control will automatically attempt reset after one hour on ignition lockout conditions. This is a better documentation anchor than spec-sheet brochure legends alone, and confirms the 1-hour auto-reset is consistent across both legacy single-LED and modern 7-segment platforms.

platforms.platform_description: Pre-2012 Goodman and Amana residential gas furnace platform with single red LED on integrated furnace control. Flash-count codes (1-8 flashes) plus steady-on and continuous/rapid polarity states. Simple namespace, no menu system, no digital display. Public flash legend published on Amana AMH95 product specification p.5 and Goodman GMH95/GCH95 spec sheets.
platforms.visual_identification: Single red LED on integrated furnace control board. No digital display. Flash codes read as count of red LED pulses: 2 through 8 flashes map to specific faults. Steady ON = normal. Off = no control signal or failure. Continuous/rapid flashing = reversed line polarity.
platforms.platform_notes: Public documentation uses a concise flash legend without detailed per-flash cause trees — the legend exposes the meaning clearly but root-cause and diagnostic-path detail is implied from general furnace service practice rather than per-code published text. Records below honestly note 'Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet' where public coverage is thin.

codes.meaning: Normal operation
root_causes: None; published as the normal operating indication
diagnostic_path: Confirm furnace is operating normally; if customer complaint persists, continue troubleshooting by symptom because the LED itself is not flagging a fault
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 product specification flash legend

codes.meaning: Internal control failure / no control signal
root_causes: No 24 V power to integrated control
diagnostic_path: Check 24 V low-voltage power at control
Replace control only after power/fuse checks are verified
codes.escalation: Control enters locked-out state if internal fault is confirmed
codes.reset_behavior: Restore power/fuse condition if external power loss caused the state; replace control if internal fault is confirmed.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart entry for no LED signal, p.45 (used for board-level interpretation of 'off' state since legacy brochure legend does not expand 'off' into a cause tree)

codes.meaning: System lockout (retries exceeded)
root_causes: Flame not sensed during ignition trials leading to lockout after repeated retries
diagnostic_path: Inspect gas interruption, igniter, flame sensor, pressure-switch proving, and induced-draft performance per applicable service manual
codes.escalation: Three ignition tries total, then lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Reset by turning thermostat off for more than 5 seconds and less than 20 seconds and back on, or by turning furnace power off for a minimum of 5 seconds; automatic reset after one hour.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 flash legend p.5; DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions p.41

codes.meaning: Pressure switch stuck closed
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Verify pressure switch is not closed at call start
Inspect switch and circuit/wiring per applicable furnace service manual
codes.escalation: Furnace heat cycle inhibited until fault is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when pressure-switch fault is corrected and control sees normal startup condition.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Pressure switch stuck open
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Inspect pressure-switch hose, vent/intake blockage, drain issues on condensing units if applicable, and pressure-switch wiring
codes.escalation: Furnace heat cycle inhibited until pressure-switch proving is restored
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when the pressure-switch circuit closes properly during heat call.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Open high limit
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
codes.escalation: Burner shutoff / heating interruption until limit closes or fault is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after limit circuit returns to normal and control allows operation.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Flame sensed without gas valve
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Verify gas valve is not leaking or lingering open
Inspect low-voltage short/ground issues as applicable to the platform
codes.escalation: Fault advisory/heat interruption until false flame condition is removed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once false flame condition is removed and control sees normal state.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Open rollout or open fuse
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Verify rollout circuit continuity and integrated fuse condition
Determine whether rollout opened from combustion/venting issue before reset or part replacement
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate until safety/fuse issue is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Restore fuse or correct rollout fault; manual rollout reset (if used on the appliance) must follow unit-specific instructions.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Low flame signal
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Verify gas pressure/combustion quality if flame carryover is weak
codes.escalation: Advisory first; can progress to ignition failure/lockout if flame sense becomes inadequate
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after flame-sense signal returns to acceptable level.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

codes.meaning: Check igniter or improper grounding
root_causes: Not explicitly detailed in accessible legacy brochure snippet
diagnostic_path: Inspect igniter wiring and control output
codes.escalation: Heat cycle inhibited until igniter/ground problem is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after normal igniter circuit and grounding are restored.
codes.source: Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend explicitly includes '8 flashes = check igniter / improper grounding'; Amana family flash legend is the cross-brand alias reference

codes.meaning: Reversed 115 VAC polarity
diagnostic_path: Verify equipment grounding and wiring correctness
codes.escalation: Furnace fault indication; operation may be inhibited or unreliable until corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once polarity/grounding issue is corrected.
codes.source: Amana AMH95 product specification flash legend p.5; Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend

platforms.platform_description: Modern Goodman, Amana, and Daikin-rebadged residential gas furnaces using dual 7-segment alphanumeric display on integrated furnace control. Covers DC96VC/DM96VC and equivalents (GMVC96, AMVC96, GMVM96, AMVM96, GMVM97, AMVM97, GCVC96, ACVC96, GMES96, AMES96). ComfortNet-capable but also operates in traditional non-communicating mode. Ignition lockout: 3 tries, 1-hour auto-reset. Fault history: last 6 with consecutive repeats capped at 3. Board REQUIRES correct shared-data memory card for communicating operation — d0 = no shared data, d4 = invalid shared data.
platforms.visual_identification: Dual 7-segment alphanumeric display on integrated furnace control. Normal operation shows '--' or blower status. E-prefix codes (E0-E9, EA) for primary faults. d-prefix codes (d0, d4) for communicating shared-data issues. b-prefix codes (b0-b5, b7, b9) for blower motor faults. ComfortNet thermostat (CTK04 or Daikin One+) may show 'Call for Service' icon or 'Check Furnace' scroll text mirroring active board code.
platforms.platform_notes: Primary public source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, pp.41-48. Ignition lockout procedure details on p.41. Diagnostics menu structure and Fault 1-Fault 6 recall on p.53. Cross-platform conflict: 'E3' here = open primary/aux/rollout limit; on Daikin Fit inverter platform 'E3' does NOT exist but 'E4' (flame sensed no call) vs Fit 'E41' (low refrigerant) create namespace confusion. b4, b5, b7 rows had incomplete public snippet coverage — full cause trees not fully exposed; records honestly note this rather than padding.

codes.meaning: Furnace lockout due to excessive number of ignition retries
root_causes: Front cover pressure switch stuck open
Coated/oxidized or improperly connected flame sensor
Lazy burner flames from improper gas pressure or restricted flue/combustion air
diagnostic_path: Check front-cover pressure switch operation and wiring
codes.escalation: Three total ignition tries, then lockout; auto-reset after one hour
codes.reset_behavior: Thermostat off for more than 5 seconds and less than 20 seconds, then back on; or disconnect power for at least 5 seconds; auto-reset after one hour.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions, abnormal operation and troubleshooting chart, pp.41, 45

codes.meaning: Low-stage pressure switch circuit is closed at start of heating cycle
diagnostic_path: Replace low-stage pressure switch if contacts are sticking
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate until startup condition is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after switch/wiring fault is corrected and startup proves normal.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.45

codes.meaning: Low-stage pressure switch circuit is not closed
root_causes: Blocked flue or inlet air pipe
Incorrect pressure-switch set point or malfunctioning contacts
diagnostic_path: Inspect flue and/or inlet air piping for blockage/length/elbows/termination
codes.escalation: Induced-draft blower runs continuously with no further furnace operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after pressure-switch proving is restored.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.46

codes.meaning: Primary limit or auxiliary limit circuit is open; rollout limit circuit is open
codes.escalation: Circulator blower runs continuously; no furnace operation until limit condition clears
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after the open limit/rollout circuit returns to normal and underlying cause is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.46

codes.meaning: Flame sensed with no call for heat
root_causes: Short to ground in flame-sense circuit
diagnostic_path: Correct short at flame sensor/flame-sensor wiring
codes.escalation: Induced-draft blower and circulator blower run continuously; no furnace operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after false-flame condition is removed.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.46

codes.meaning: Open fuse
diagnostic_path: Locate and correct short in low-voltage wiring
codes.reset_behavior: Restore fuse after short is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.46

codes.meaning: Flame sense microamp signal is low
root_causes: Flame sensor incorrectly positioned in burner flame
Lazy burner flame due to improper gas pressure or combustion air
diagnostic_path: Compare gas pressure to rating plate and adjust as needed
codes.escalation: Furnace may continue to run with reduced flame-sense margin; can progress to ignition failure/lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when flame-sense signal returns to acceptable range.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.46

codes.meaning: Problem with igniter circuit
root_causes: Igniter relay fault on integrated control module
diagnostic_path: Check/correct wiring from control to igniter
Check igniter output from control and replace control if necessary
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after igniter circuit fault is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.47

codes.meaning: High-stage pressure-switch circuit is closed at start of heating cycle; furnace operates in low stage only
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate on high stage and runs low stage only
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after switch/wiring fault is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.47

codes.meaning: High-stage pressure-switch circuit is not closed; furnace operates in low stage only
root_causes: Incorrect pressure-switch set point or malfunctioning contacts
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate on high stage and remains in low stage only
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after high-stage pressure-switch proving is restored.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.47

codes.meaning: Polarity of 115 V AC is reversed
root_causes: Reversed line polarity to furnace or control module
diagnostic_path: Review wiring diagram to correct polarity
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate until corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after polarity/ground issue is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.47

codes.meaning: Data not yet on network / no net data
root_causes: Furnace does not contain any shared data
diagnostic_path: Populate shared data set using memory card
Use memory card for the specific model
Insert memory card before turning power on
Remove only after data is loaded and power is turned off
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate in communicating context until required shared data is present
codes.reset_behavior: Error clears once data is loaded.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, pp.47-48

codes.meaning: Invalid memory-card data
root_causes: Shared data set on memory card rejected by integrated control module
diagnostic_path: Verify shared data set is correct for the specific model
Repopulate using correct memory card if required
codes.escalation: Operation different than expected or no operation until valid shared data is accepted
codes.reset_behavior: Error clears once valid data is loaded and power is cycled per procedure.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Circulator blower motor is not running when it should be running
root_causes: Loose wiring connection at blower motor power leads or disconnected leads
Open circuit in inductor or loose wiring connection at inductor (3/4 HP and 1 HP models only)
diagnostic_path: Verify continuity through inductor and replace if open/short
Check circulator blower motor and replace if necessary
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after blower-motor power fault is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Integrated control module has lost communications with circulator blower motor
root_causes: Loose wiring connection at blower motor control leads
diagnostic_path: Check blower motor and replace if necessary
Check integrated control module and replace if necessary
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after blower communication path is restored.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Circulator blower motor horsepower in shared data set does not match blower motor horsepower
root_causes: Incorrect shared-data set in integrated control module
diagnostic_path: Verify blower motor horsepower is correct for furnace model
Verify shared data set is correct for model and repopulate using correct memory card if required
codes.reset_behavior: Error clears once shared data and motor horsepower match.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Circulator blower motor is operating in a power, temperature, or speed limiting condition
diagnostic_path: Verify all registers are fully open
Verify ductwork is appropriately sized and resize/replace if necessary
codes.escalation: Furnace operates at reduced performance; airflow delivered is less than expected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after limiting condition is removed.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Motor trips
root_causes: Accessible public snippet confirms the code and failure state, but the full cause list is not fully exposed in the accessible snippet
diagnostic_path: Turn power off prior to repair
Use full troubleshooting chart in the public service manual for the unit-specific cause tree
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly detailed in accessible public snippet.
codes.source: DM80VC/DC80VC installation manual troubleshooting chart snippet and DC96VC-family troubleshooting section confirm b4 'motor trips', but the complete row was not fully exposed in the accessible snippet

codes.meaning: Blower motor rotor locked
root_causes: Accessible public snippet confirms the code meaning, but the full row with detailed causes/actions was not fully exposed in the accessible snippet
diagnostic_path: Turn power off prior to repair
Use full public troubleshooting chart in the service manual for the complete cause tree
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly detailed in accessible public snippet.
codes.source: Goodman ComfortNet modular blower public manual lists b5 as 'blower motor rotor locked'; DC96VC-family platform uses the same blower fault namespace, but the complete DC96VC row was not fully exposed in the accessible snippet

codes.meaning: Motor parameters fault / incomplete parameter sent to motor
root_causes: Accessible public snippet confirms the code meaning, but the complete cause list for the furnace row was not fully exposed in the accessible snippet
diagnostic_path: Verify correct parameter/shared-data configuration and blower/control compatibility using the applicable public service manual
codes.escalation: Furnace fails to operate or operates with reduced performance depending on fault state
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly detailed in accessible public snippet.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC troubleshooting section confirms b7 'MOTOR PARAMS'; Goodman ComfortNet modular blower public manual lists b7 as 'incomplete parameter sent to motor'

codes.meaning: Low indoor airflow
diagnostic_path: Check ductwork for restriction or obstruction
Correct duct sizing issue as required
codes.escalation: Furnace operates at reduced performance or airflow below requirement
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when airflow deficiency is corrected.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, p.48

codes.meaning: Auxiliary switch open
root_causes: Wiring fault on auxiliary switch circuit
Heat exchanger or cabinet temperature issue triggering auxiliary limit
diagnostic_path: Check auxiliary switch continuity and reset if possible
Inspect wiring on auxiliary limit circuit
Investigate underlying temperature/airflow cause before clearing
codes.escalation: Furnace operation inhibited until auxiliary switch closes or fault is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after auxiliary switch circuit returns to normal.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; public furnace status-code reference

codes.meaning: Over/under-voltage trip or over-temperature trip on blower motor
root_causes: Line voltage outside motor operating range (too high or too low)
Bearing or load condition causing overheating
diagnostic_path: Measure line voltage at furnace and compare to motor nameplate operating range
Check blower motor amp draw and compare to nameplate
Inspect blower wheel for obstruction or bearing issues
Verify motor cooling path is not obstructed
codes.escalation: Blower motor trips; furnace fails to operate until voltage/temperature condition is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after voltage/temperature condition returns to normal.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; public furnace status-code reference

codes.meaning: Normal operation — no active fault
root_causes: Furnace operating normally; board self-check passed
diagnostic_path: If symptom persists with 'On' displayed, continue troubleshooting by symptom because the board itself is not flagging a fault
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; alternates with numeric airflow value during operation

codes.meaning: Active cooling — low stage (first-stage cool)
root_causes: Normal operation: thermostat cooling call on Y1 stage
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify thermostat is not calling for cooling
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; alternates with airflow CFM value

codes.meaning: Active cooling — high stage (second-stage cool)
root_causes: Normal operation: thermostat cooling call on Y2 stage
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify thermostat is not calling for high-stage cooling
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; alternates with airflow CFM value

codes.meaning: Active heat-pump heat — low stage (first-stage heat pump)
root_causes: Normal operation: heat-pump heating call on Y1 stage
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify heat pump is actually running and thermostat is calling
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; applies when furnace is part of dual-fuel or heat-pump system

codes.meaning: Active heat-pump heat — high stage (second-stage heat pump)
root_causes: Normal operation: heat-pump heating call on Y2 stage
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify heat pump is actually running and thermostat is calling for high stage
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; applies when furnace is part of dual-fuel or heat-pump system

codes.meaning: Active gas heat — low stage (first-stage gas heat)
root_causes: Normal operation: low-stage gas heat call and firing
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify thermostat is not calling for heat
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; indicates first-stage gas valve energized

codes.meaning: Active gas heat — high stage (second-stage gas heat)
root_causes: Normal operation: high-stage gas heat call and firing
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify high-stage call from thermostat
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page; indicates second-stage gas valve energized

codes.meaning: Continuous fan mode — blower running without heating or cooling call
root_causes: Normal operation: thermostat G call active without W or Y call
diagnostic_path: If unexpected, verify thermostat fan switch position
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions status code page

platforms.platform_description: Daikin/Goodman/Amana communicating ecosystem surfacing equipment faults through CTK04 ComfortNet thermostat and Daikin One+ smart thermostat. Public documentation confirms multi-surface architecture: thermostat shows 'Call for Service' icon and 'Check Furnace' scroll text; equipment boards retain detailed E/d/b code display. Daikin One+ exposes Error History under System Optimization menu with color-coded alerts (white = not critical, yellow = critical). Public thermostat-native code dictionary is thin — most fault detail lives at equipment level. Fault history: last 6 with consecutive repeats capped at 3.
platforms.visual_identification: CTK04 ComfortNet thermostat is a small monochrome display with equipment diagnostics menus. Daikin One+ is a color touchscreen smart thermostat with System Optimization menu including Error History. Both thermostat types show 'Call for Service' icon when equipment has active fault.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source coverage is thinner for Platform 3 than Platforms 1-2 or 4. DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions pp.45-48 confirm 'Call for Service' and 'Check Furnace' appear with equipment faults. Daikin One+ S21 Installation Guide p.28 confirms Error History menu. Goodman ComfortNet modular blower p.12 confirms LEARN-button reset and LED status interpretation. Public docs don't expose a rich thermostat-native code dictionary comparable to equipment board charts — records here honestly represent what IS publicly documented without padding.

codes.meaning: Thermostat-level service alert indicating active equipment fault
root_causes: Mirrors active equipment fault from connected indoor or outdoor unit
Public docs show it paired with board codes such as E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, E7, E8, E9, d0, d4, b0, b1, b2, b3, b7
diagnostic_path: Read active equipment-board code on indoor/outdoor unit
Use thermostat service alert as pointer, not the final fault definition
Use the applicable equipment troubleshooting chart
codes.escalation: Service alert remains while active equipment fault is present
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when underlying equipment fault clears.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart entries repeatedly show ComfortNet thermostat 'Call for Service' icon illuminated for active furnace faults, pp.45-48

codes.meaning: Thermostat scroll message indicating the connected furnace has an active fault requiring service lookup at equipment level
root_causes: Mirrors active furnace faults such as E0 through E9, d0, d4, b0 through b3, b7, b9
diagnostic_path: Go to furnace board dual 7-segment display
codes.escalation: Service-call state while active equipment fault is present
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when the underlying furnace fault clears.
codes.source: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions troubleshooting chart, multiple entries on pp.45-48

codes.meaning: Daikin One+ System Optimization menu item that logs alerts with an error code
root_causes: Not a fault itself; menu for reviewing stored equipment alerts
Public guide states entries are color-coded white = not critical, yellow = critical
diagnostic_path: Navigate to System Optimization > Error History
Review logged alerts and their error codes
Inspect corresponding indoor/outdoor equipment status and service literature
codes.escalation: None by itself; depends on the logged equipment alert
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly detailed in accessible public snippet.
codes.source: Daikin One+ S21 Installation Guide, Step 4 — System Optimization, p.28; Daikin One+ public installation/commissioning literature also describes Error History under System Optimization

codes.meaning: Diagnostic history menu showing last six faults, most recent to least recent; CLEAR deletes stored history
root_causes: Not a fault itself; record structure for stored equipment faults
diagnostic_path: Read Fault 1 through Fault 6 in order
Use CLEAR = YES to erase history after service
codes.reset_behavior: Selecting YES clears fault history.
codes.source: DM96VC/DC96VC installation literature diagnostics menu, p.53; Goodman ComfortNet modular blower advanced-features menu, pp.11-12 equivalent structure

codes.meaning: Network reset function on communicating indoor control
root_causes: Used when troubleshooting network communication issues
diagnostic_path: Use onboard network tools: red communications LED, green Rx LED, and LEARN button
Depress button for approximately 2 seconds to reset network on indoor communicating control
codes.escalation: None by itself; used during network troubleshooting
codes.reset_behavior: Depress approximately 2 seconds for network reset on indoor ComfortNet controls; the same public table also distinguishes quick press vs hold for out-of-box reset on some indoor communicating controls.
codes.source: Goodman ComfortNet modular blower public installation manual, network troubleshooting and communications status LEDs, p.12

platforms.platform_description: Daikin Fit inverter-driven outdoor unit platform. Distinct engineering lineage from Goodman/Amana furnace platform — uses numeric E-code namespace (E25-E59, B0/B9, D0-D4) plus thermostat-only E11. 3-digit onboard PCB display with LEARN/RECALL/TEST button service workflow (Screen 0-4 modes). Network troubleshooting via red communications LED (H1P/H2P) and green receive LED (H3P). Cross-platform conflict: Fit E41 = low refrigerant, while Platform 2 E4 = flame sensed no call — same E-prefix, completely different fault families. US residential Fit does NOT use L-codes or U-codes (that Japanese-style namespace is commercial/mini-split territory).
platforms.visual_identification: Outdoor unit has 3-digit alphanumeric PCB display visible through service panel. LEARN / RECALL / TEST buttons on PCB. H1P and H2P red communications LEDs, H3P green receive LED. Numeric codes display with or without E-prefix (e.g., '25' or 'E25'). Letter-prefix codes B0/B9 display as 'B0'/'B9' or 'Eb0'/'Eb9'; D-codes as 'D0'-'D4' or 'Ed0'-'Ed4'. Thermostat (Daikin One+) shows E11 during commissioning/test workflow.
platforms.platform_notes: Primary public source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table pp.35-37, network LED table pp.37-38, Mode Display Introduction pp.38-40. Platform uses the same shared-data memory-card architecture as Platform 2 furnaces (D0/D1/D3/D4 mirror Platform 2 d0/d4). L/U codes are NOT part of US residential Fit namespace — any encounter indicates a different Daikin product line.

codes.meaning: Outdoor air temperature sensor is open or shorted
root_causes: Faulty outdoor thermistor sensor or disconnect
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after sensor circuit returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control determines that the pressure sensor is not reacting properly
root_causes: Pressure sensor inoperable or not properly connected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after pressure-sensor response returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Outdoor coil defrost temperature sensor is out of range
root_causes: Outdoor defrost thermistor inoperable or not properly connected
diagnostic_path: Check connection to outdoor defrost thermistor
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after sensor input returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Outdoor coil temperature sensor is out of range
root_causes: Outdoor coil thermistor inoperable or not properly connected
diagnostic_path: Check connection to outdoor coil thermistor
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after sensor input returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Liquid temperature sensor is out of range
root_causes: Liquid thermistor inoperable or not properly connected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after sensor input returns to normal.

codes.meaning: Indicates the control board may need to be replaced
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after wiring/board issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Equipment is experiencing high-temperature faults on the outdoor unit control board
root_causes: Cooling bracket screw(s) missing/not properly fastened in very low ambient condition
No/poor thermal grease coating between cooling plumbing and cooling bracket on control board
No/low/limited flow through control-board cooling circuit from line restriction or low refrigerant
diagnostic_path: Cycle power and retry in usable ambient range
Check grease application in very low ambient condition
Verify stop valve fully open and repair if needed
codes.escalation: Fault indication; public table does not give an explicit retry/lockout timer in accessible snippet
codes.reset_behavior: Retry after correction; automatic clearing behavior not explicitly timed in accessible snippet.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control board detected a high current condition; indicates potential short circuit
diagnostic_path: Check power supply for in-rush current during startup/steady state
Check wire between control board and compressor
Check compressor and repair/replace if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after root cause is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control board detected a high current condition
diagnostic_path: Check compressor and repair/replace if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after root cause is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control encountered an abnormal condition during startup procedure
root_causes: Blocked/restricted outdoor unit coil and/or lines
diagnostic_path: Check/clean outdoor unit coil and/or lines
Check wire between control board and compressor
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after startup condition normalizes and cause is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Indicates the control board may need to be replaced
root_causes: Outdoor fan motor not connected properly
diagnostic_path: Check wiring from outdoor fan motor to control board
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after wiring/board issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control has detected a voltage-related issue with the compressor
root_causes: High or low voltage from supply
diagnostic_path: Check wire between control board and compressor
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after voltage/compressor wiring issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Indicates the control board may need to be replaced
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermistor/board fault is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control determines its compressor requirement is different than the compressor capability
diagnostic_path: Check memory-card data versus outdoor unit model
Verify control-board size versus outdoor unit model
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after correct data/control is installed.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control board has detected a low refrigerant condition
root_causes: Thermistors inoperable or not properly connected
diagnostic_path: Test for system leaks using leak-test procedure
Check refrigerant charge level and adjust if needed
Check connection to thermistor and repair/replace if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after refrigerant condition/sensor issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control detects a low power-supply voltage condition
diagnostic_path: Verify unit is connected to power supply as specified on rating plate
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after supply voltage returns to range.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.35

codes.meaning: Control detects a high power-supply voltage condition
diagnostic_path: Verify unit is connected to power supply as specified on rating plate
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after supply voltage returns to range.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.36

codes.meaning: Control detects outdoor temperature outside recommended operating range; unit may continue to operate normally
root_causes: Ambient air conditions too high or too low
diagnostic_path: Retry during usable ambient temperature range
codes.escalation: Advisory/protection message; public text says unit may continue to operate normally
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when ambient condition returns to acceptable range.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.36

codes.meaning: Control is unable to start Cooling mode test because indoor heat has been turned on by thermostat; set thermostat to off position
root_causes: Heat provided by secondary heating source
diagnostic_path: Turn off furnace/heater using thermostat before operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermostat is set correctly and indoor heat is off.

codes.meaning: Control is unable to start System Verification test because indoor heat has been turned on by thermostat; set thermostat to off position
root_causes: Heat provided by secondary heating source
diagnostic_path: Turn off furnace/heater using thermostat before operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermostat is set correctly and indoor heat is off.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.36

codes.meaning: Control is unable to enter Charging Mode because indoor heat has been turned on by thermostat; set thermostat to off position
root_causes: Heat provided by secondary heating source
diagnostic_path: Turn off heater using thermostat before operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermostat is set correctly and indoor heat is off.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.36

codes.meaning: Voltage issue on control board; see service manual for troubleshooting information
root_causes: High or low voltage from supply voltage or frequency
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after supply/board issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.36

codes.meaning: Potential communication issues detected by outdoor unit control board
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after communication is restored.

codes.meaning: Outdoor suction temperature sensor is out of range
root_causes: Suction thermistor inoperable or not properly connected

codes.reset_behavior: Clears after sensor/valve issue is corrected.

codes.meaning: Overload protection sensor for compressor is opened
root_causes: Jumper wire (X33A) in incorrect position or off
diagnostic_path: Check OL sensor position on compressor body
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after overload sensor/jumper condition is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Equipment is experiencing reversing-valve faults
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after valve/refrigerant/sensor condition is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Estimated airflow from indoor subsystem is near 0 CFM
root_causes: Indoor fan motor not properly connected
codes.escalation: Indoor airflow failure affecting outdoor operation
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after indoor airflow path is restored.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Estimated airflow from motor is lower than airflow requirement
root_causes: Indoor fan motor not properly connected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after airflow returns to requirement.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Control board does not have the necessary data for it to properly perform its functions
root_causes: Outdoor unit is wired as part of a communicating system and integrated control module does not contain shared data
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after proper data/control condition is restored.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Control board does not have the appropriate data needed to properly perform its functions
root_causes: Outdoor unit is wired as part of a communicating system
Invalid shared data or network data is invalid for the integrated control module
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after proper data/control condition is restored.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Airflow requirement is greater than the airflow capability of the indoor subsystem
root_causes: Outdoor unit requires greater airflow than indoor capability or type of indoor unit with EEV is connected
Shared data incompatible with system or missing parameters
Communication wiring with indoor unit has loose connection
diagnostic_path: Check combination against rating list and correct if needed
Verify shared data for specific model and repopulate if required
Check communication and power-supply wiring of indoor unit and repair as needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after proper indoor/outdoor match/data/wiring is restored.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Mismatch between shared data and the control physical hardware
root_causes: Shared data sent to integrated control module does not match hardware configuration
diagnostic_path: Verify shared data is correct for the specific model
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after correct shared data is loaded.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: Memory-card data has been rejected
root_causes: Shared data on memory card has been rejected
diagnostic_path: Verify shared data is correct for the specific model
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after valid memory-card data is accepted.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, troubleshooting table, p.37

codes.meaning: System start-up test is required or in progress; installer should navigate to thermostat menu to run system start-up test; code clears once testing is complete
diagnostic_path: Run the SYSTEM START-UP TEST from thermostat/install setup following outdoor unit installation manual
codes.reset_behavior: Clears automatically once testing is complete.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, thermostat-only message row, p.37

codes.meaning: Normal condition
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, p.38

codes.meaning: Communications failure
codes.reset_behavior: Reset network using LEARN button, approximately 5 seconds.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table and LEARN-button note, p.38

codes.meaning: Out-of-box reset
diagnostic_path: None listed as required in table
Confirm whether this is expected during setup/reset activity
codes.escalation: Reset-state indicator, not a fault by itself
codes.reset_behavior: State occurs on control power-up or learn-button action.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, p.38

codes.meaning: No power or communications error
diagnostic_path: Check for shorts in low-voltage wiring
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after power/communication problem is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, p.38

codes.meaning: No network found
diagnostic_path: Check communication wires terminal 1/terminal 2 and replace if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when network is established.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, pp.37-38

codes.meaning: Normal network traffic
root_causes: Control is 'talking' on network as expected
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, p.37

codes.meaning: Terminal 1 / Terminal 2 mis-wire
root_causes: Terminal 1 and Terminal 2 reversed at indoor unit/thermostat/outdoor unit
Short between terminal 1 or 2 and terminal C or R
diagnostic_path: Check communication wires terminal 1/terminal 2 and replace if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after miswire/short is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, network troubleshooting LED table, pp.37-38

codes.meaning: Repeated high-temperature faults on outdoor control board; continued operation acceptable
root_causes: Repeated brief high-temp events on control board during operation
Marginal cooling of control board under load
diagnostic_path: Confirm ambient operating conditions are within range
Check control-board cooling path (grease, screws, airflow)
Check for refrigerant-side restrictions affecting board cooling
codes.escalation: Advisory — manual explicitly states continued operation is acceptable
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermal stress event frequency drops back to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

codes.meaning: Frequent compressor faults; continued operation acceptable
root_causes: Compressor showing signs of stress but not failing to start
diagnostic_path: Check compressor amp draw and starting behavior
Schedule compressor replacement if trend continues
codes.escalation: Advisory — continued operation acceptable but system is flagging a real equipment problem
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after compressor fault frequency returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

codes.meaning: Frequent outdoor control-board and/or motor faults; continued operation acceptable
diagnostic_path: Inspect outdoor fan motor for bearing/load issues
codes.escalation: Advisory — continued operation acceptable but indicates a real developing problem
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fault frequency returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

codes.meaning: Frequent low-discharge-superheat faults; continued operation acceptable
root_causes: Recurring low superheat at compressor discharge
EEV control issue causing periodic flooding
diagnostic_path: Verify refrigerant charge level against manufacturer specification
Verify suction and discharge temperatures against expected envelope
codes.escalation: Advisory — continued operation acceptable but compressor is periodically seeing liquid return
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after superheat frequency returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

codes.meaning: Frequent high-discharge-temperature faults; continued operation acceptable
diagnostic_path: Verify condenser fan motor operation and speed
codes.escalation: Advisory — continued operation acceptable but compressor is periodically running hot
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after high-temperature frequency returns to normal.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

codes.meaning: Sweating in cooling loop at low ambient conditions
root_causes: EEV issue affecting superheat control at low ambient
Thermistor issue causing incorrect EEV response
diagnostic_path: Check EEV operation and thermistor readings
Verify suction and discharge temperatures at low ambient
codes.escalation: Advisory — flags condensation risk in cooling loop at low ambient
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after underlying leak/charge/EEV/thermistor issue is corrected.
codes.source: Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S, advisory code section

platforms.platform_description: Goodman/Daikin ComfortNet-capable outdoor condenser and heat pump platform PREDATING Daikin Fit inverter. Covers DX16TC, DZ16TC, DX18TC, DZ18TC — single-stage and two-stage communicating outdoor units that use shared-data memory card architecture but are NOT inverter-driven. Public service literature exposes distinct code namespace: d0/d4 for communicating shared-data (same concept as furnace d0/d4 but at outdoor-unit level), 01/L1 for low-side/low-pressure lockout, 02 for high-side fault, 06 for open start circuit, 07 for open run circuit. CRITICAL: Do NOT confuse with Daikin Fit (Platform 4) — Fit uses E25-E59 numeric namespace and IS inverter; DX16TC/DZ16TC use this 01/L1/02/06/07 namespace and are NOT inverter.
platforms.visual_identification: Outdoor unit PCB displays communicating d0/d4/01/L1/02/06/07 codes. Unit is communicating-capable (shared-data memory card) but NOT inverter-driven — visually has conventional two-stage compressor hardware rather than Fit's inverter module.
platforms.platform_notes: Public service literature source. Reset behavior: low-side (01/L1) lockout can auto-clear after 30 consecutive normal cycles OR be cleared by cycling 24 VAC to the outdoor control. This distinct clearing mechanism (30-cycle auto-recovery) is unique to this platform — not seen on Platform 4 Fit.

codes.meaning: Data not yet on network (outdoor unit has no shared data)
root_causes: Outdoor unit is wired as part of a communicating system but integrated control module does not contain shared data
diagnostic_path: Populate shared data set using memory card
Use memory card for the specific model
Insert memory card before turning power on
Remove only after data is loaded and power is turned off
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit fails to operate in communicating context until shared data is present
codes.reset_behavior: Error clears once data is loaded.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin DX16TC/DZ16TC ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

codes.meaning: Invalid memory-card data (outdoor unit)
root_causes: Shared data set on memory card rejected by outdoor integrated control module
diagnostic_path: Verify shared data set is correct for specific outdoor unit model
Repopulate using correct memory card if required
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit operation different than expected or inhibited until valid shared data is accepted
codes.reset_behavior: Error clears once valid data is loaded and power is cycled per procedure.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin DX16TC/DZ16TC ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

codes.meaning: Low-side fault / low-pressure lockout
diagnostic_path: Inspect indoor coil and filter for restriction
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-clears after 30 consecutive normal cycles OR manually cleared by cycling 24 VAC to the outdoor control.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

codes.meaning: High-side fault / high-pressure fault
diagnostic_path: Verify condenser fan motor operation and speed
Evacuate and recharge if non-condensables suspected
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit locks out compressor on high-pressure event
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-clears after fault condition clears and anti-short-cycle timer completes OR manually cleared by cycling 24 VAC.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

codes.meaning: Open start circuit
root_causes: Start winding or start capacitor circuit open
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after start circuit fault is corrected.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

codes.meaning: Open run circuit
root_causes: Run winding or run capacitor circuit open
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after run circuit fault is corrected.
codes.source: Goodman/Daikin ComfortNet outdoor unit public service literature

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Same number, different meanings across platform generations

meanings.meaning: Legacy '3 flashes' = pressure switch stuck open
Modern 'E3' = primary/aux/rollout limit circuit open (completely different diagnostic family)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Pressure switch stuck open on legacy boards flashes 3 times — but 'E3' on modern 7-segment boards is an OPEN LIMIT fault, not a pressure switch fault. Identify the control surface first: single red LED = legacy flash codes (2 flashes=pressure stuck closed, 3=pressure stuck open, 4=limit); dual 7-segment display = E-codes (E2=low-stage pressure not closed, E3=limit open, E4=flame sensed no call).

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Same E-prefix namespace but completely different meanings across Goodman furnace vs Daikin Fit inverter
cross_generation_conflicts.code_identifier: Modern furnace E4 vs Daikin Fit E41

meanings.meaning: Furnace E4 = flame sensed with no call for heat (safety-critical gas valve/flame-sense issue)
Fit E41 = low refrigerant condition detected by control board (refrigerant leak, low charge, or faulty thermistor)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: The 'E' prefix on Daikin-owned equipment does NOT mean the same thing across platforms. Furnace E4 is a gas-valve/flame-sense safety issue. Daikin Fit E41 is low refrigerant. Different engineering lineages, different meanings. ALWAYS confirm the surface (indoor furnace board vs outdoor Fit PCB) before interpreting any E-code.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Same event shows different identifiers on equipment board vs ComfortNet thermostat surface
cross_generation_conflicts.code_identifier: Equipment board code vs thermostat service message

meanings.meaning: Equipment 7-segment board shows specific code like E0, E1, E2, d0, d4, b1 with detailed fault meaning
Same event surfaces at CTK04 thermostat only as 'Call for Service' icon or 'Check Furnace' scroll text — the equipment-level fault detail is NOT published at the thermostat

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: ComfortNet thermostat showing 'Call for Service' or 'Check Furnace' is a POINTER — it tells you there's an active equipment fault but not WHAT. Always go to the equipment's 7-segment display (E0-E9, EA, d0, d4, b0-b9) to read the actual code. Use Daikin One+ Error History under System Optimization to review recent logged alerts with color coding (white = not critical, yellow = critical).

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Two completely different outdoor-unit code namespaces within same corporate family

meanings.meaning: DX16TC/DZ16TC/DX18TC/DZ18TC (non-inverter communicating): codes are 01/L1 (low-side lockout), 02 (high-side fault), 06 (open start), 07 (open run), d0/d4 (shared data). Auto-clear after 30 consecutive normal cycles on low-side.
DZ17VSA/DZ18VSA/DX17/DX18/DZ20/DX20 (inverter): codes are E25-E59 numeric, B0/B9, D0-D4 (Ed0-Ed4), thermostat-only E11. No 30-cycle auto-clear mechanism; reset via power-cycle or LEARN button.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Daikin/Goodman has TWO distinct outdoor communicating platforms with completely different code namespaces. DX16TC/DZ16TC/DX18TC (non-inverter, pre-2020) uses 01/L1/02/06/07. Daikin Fit (DZ17VSA/DZ18VSA/DZ20 inverter, 2019+) uses E25-E59. Check the outdoor model number first — matching codes to the wrong platform will send you down the wrong diagnostic path entirely.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: Daikin/Goodman/Amana electric-heat codes surface primarily through communicating air handler controls (AVPTC, ASPT, MBVC modular blower). On ComfortNet communicating systems, electric-heat faults appear as b-code family (b0-b5, b7, b9) on the modular blower control, surfacing through thermostat 'Call for Service' icon and 'Check Furnace' scroll text. Non-communicating Goodman/Amana electric furnaces use conventional sequencer/element logic without formal fault code dictionaries — service is component-level.
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Open element on multi-stage EH packages (3-stage, 5-stage, 10-stage)
Failed sequencer on non-communicating air handlers
Airflow-related limit trip — on 7-segment modern IFC this throws E3
24V transformer or fuse failure (modern IFC throws E5 open fuse)
Low-voltage wiring short causing repeated fuse failure
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Goodman has a manufactured-home furnace line (GMPX family) but public fault code documentation for MH-specific models is thinner than standard residential. Fault codes on MH Goodman platforms typically follow the legacy single-LED flash pattern unless the unit has been upgraded to a 7-segment board.

metadata.source_note: Compiled from public Daikin/Goodman/Amana documentation across three research passes: DC96VC/DM96VC Service Instructions pp.41-48 (modern 7-segment IFC code table and diagnostic menus) plus status-code page (EF, b6, On, C1, C2, P1, P2, L0, HI, F operating status codes); Amana AMH95 product specification p.5 (legacy single-LED flash legend); Goodman GMH95/GCH95 public flash legend; Goodman GMH8/GMS8/GDH8/GDS8 installation instructions (legacy 1-hour auto-reset anchor); Daikin Fit Outdoor Unit Installation Manual 3P515859-5S pp.35-40 (full E-code table, network LED table, Mode Display workflow, DS1 dip-switch reset workflow, advisory codes 33/52/53/54/55/57); Daikin One+ S21 Installation Guide p.28 (System Optimization / Error History menu; 0.6 VDC data validation; PWR LED green flash on reversed power); Goodman ComfortNet modular blower installation manual p.12 (network troubleshooting LEDs and LEARN button timing); Goodman/Daikin DX16TC/DZ16TC ComfortNet outdoor unit service literature (Platform 5 codes: d0/d4/01/L1/02/06/07 with 30-cycle auto-clear). Pass 1 produced methodology summary. Pass 2 produced 78 structured records. Pass 3 extreme deep-dive added 10 Platform 2 records (EF, b6 + 8 operating status codes), 6 Platform 4 advisory records (33, 52, 53, 54, 55, 57), 6 Platform 5 records (new ComfortNet outdoor unitary family), 3 field quirks, and 1 cross-gen conflict. Honest gaps unchanged from Pass 2: L-codes and U-codes NOT in US residential Daikin Fit; b4/b5/b7 public snippets incomplete; CTK04/Daikin One+ thermostat-native dictionary remains thin.
